title: Smartphone main board (SLP)
body: A smartphone logic board is a substrate-like PCB (SLP / mSAP, 10+ layers, semi-additive fine lines) with DOUBLE-SIDED SMT — components on both faces (two reflow passes; large BGAs/connectors usually on one side). The AP is a PoP stack; memory (UFS NAND) and PMIC nearby; RF front-end modules and dozens of 01005 passives fill both sides. Must-label: SLP, PoP, shield can. Not to scale.

title: PoP (Package-on-Package)
body: PoP stacks a memory package on top of the logic (AP) package, joined by a ring of PoP solder balls — saves board area and shortens AP↔DRAM routing. Bottom = fan-out or flip-chip logic, molded with THROUGH-MOLD VIAS (TMV) that bring the top-package balls down to the substrate. Top = memory BGA. Must show: two ball tiers (bottom BGA + PoP balls).

title: EMI shield can
body: A shield can is a grounded metal lid over noise-sensitive blocks (RF, AP). It solders/clips onto a ground-pad FENCE that rings the covered area; often a two-piece frame+cover so the cover can be removed for rework. Draw as a bracket (lid + side walls) enclosing the parts it covers, walls landing on the board ground pads. Conformal shielding is an alternative: a sputtered metal film on the package surface (no separate can) — show as an outline.

title: Double-sided SMT
body: Both board faces carry components. Reflow runs twice: the first (bottom) side's joints are held by solder surface tension / adhesive during the second pass. Heavy parts (big BGAs, connectors) go on the top side or are glued. In a cross-section, show parts above AND below the board.

title: BTB connector & flex
body: Board-to-board (BTB) connectors link the main board to the display, battery, and camera flex cables — low mating height, many fine pins. Flex/FPC is a thin polymer ribbon that bends off-board. Draw BTB as a low body with pin teeth; flex as a thin ribbon leaving the figure edge.

title: Underfill / corner bond
body: Mobile drop reliability: the AP and large BGAs get capillary underfill (full) or corner bond (epoxy only at the corners) to resist board flex and drop shock. Show underfill fillets under flip-chip dies.

title: Smartwatch SiP
body: A smartwatch collapses the whole main board into a single System-in-Package (S-SiP): tens of bare dies + hundreds of passives mounted on ONE substrate, then a single OVERMOLD, with external LGA/BGA to the set board. Purpose: miniaturization, water resistance, drop reliability. Bare dies are MIXED wire-bond + flip-chip; ultra-small 01005 passives; through-mold vias (TMV) for top-side shielding/ground. Must-label: SiP, mold, LGA.

title: LGA vs BGA
body: BGA = solder balls on the package underside (reflow to board). LGA = flat lands (no balls) joined by board-side solder paste — lower height, common for modules/SiP. In a cross-section, BGA shows spheres, LGA shows flat pads/short bumps under the substrate.

title: Sandwich (stacked) main board
body: Recent phones stack two boards face-to-face via a spacer/interposer board so components sit in the cavity between them — doubles component area in the same footprint. (Board-level view; full stacked detail is backlog.)

title: RF front-end module
body: An RF FEM is itself a small SiP — power amplifier + filters (SAW/BAW) + switch in one molded module on the main board. Treat as a mounted module (its internals can be a recursive assembly if detailed).

title: Stacked BSI image sensor
body: Modern CMOS image sensors are back-side illuminated (BSI) and stacked: the pixel wafer is hybrid-bonded (Cu-Cu) to a logic wafer. Top-to-bottom: microlens array → color filter (Bayer RGB) → BSI photodiode/pixel layer → BEOL → hybrid bond → logic die. Packaged in a cavity under a glass lid. Must-label: microlens, color filter, hybrid bond.

title: V-groove fiber attach
body: Fibers are held in etched V-grooves (a fiber array unit, FAU) for passive alignment, then edge-butt-coupled to the PIC's waveguide with index-matching epoxy; a glass lid clamps them. Beam height must match the waveguide. Draw a trapezoidal groove with the round fiber seated in it.

title: Butterfly laser module
body: A telecom laser sits epi-down (flip-chip/solder) on an AlN submount on a thermo-electric cooler (TEC); a lens collimates the edge-emitted beam to a fiber; a monitor photodiode behind the laser tracks power. Draw submount + laser + lens + fiber with a horizontal beam.

title: Microlens array
body: A microlens array (MLA) is a row of dome lenses that focus light onto each pixel (image sensors) or shape VCSEL beams (3D sensing). Draw as a row of hemispheres on top of the color filter / emitter.
